children.text:
"Somos Semilla" Chorus
We are seeds, we are the fruit of bomba  
We are the flowers  
We are new growth  
We are the bud, and our inheritance is the drums  
Verses
From a beautiful tree that gave us shade  
We inherited these traditions  
And day by day we learn bomba 
The drums, the dance, and the songs  

We are the legacy of our ancestors  
Who left their mark in our hearts  
We are the echo of the barrels  

We carry Puerto Rican blood  
From cimarronas and cimarrones  
We always carry our culture  
We defend it with a thousand honors  

We are the present, we are the future  
Seedbeds of generations  
Daughters and sons of our bomba  
And we share our gifts well  

We will keep playing bomba throughout the world and all its corners  
With the barrels, with maracas  
With joy and with a thousand loves

"Cuando baila la negrita" 
Chorus When the little Black girl dances  
Thunder, lightning, drum  
When the little Black girl dances  
Rain, rhythm—  
She got wet  

When the little Black girl dances, the drum keeps time  
When the little Black girl dances—rain, sky, drum  
Chorus  
The little Black girl dances bomba, yuba inspired her  
Every strike of the drum lit up the heart  

When the little Black girl dances, the stars shine in chic  
She danced bomba in Don Pedro’s little house, and it rang out  

When the little Black girl dances—drum, rain, in the wind  
She got wet; the drums tell stories  
Of courage and valor—Puerto Rico, I love you  
And I sing to you from the heart  

When the little Black girl dances—thunder, gold, drum  
When the little Black girl dances, she rises to the wind, she got wet  

When the little Black girl dances, the lightning dances  
When the little joyful girl dances, in the wind she got wet  
The little Black girl dances bomba, inspired by love  
It ignited in the heart

"Soy los elementos" am earth I am airI am waterI am fireWhen I enter the batey I free myself
I am earthI am airI am waterI am fireWhen I enter the batey I free myself

I am riverI am mountainI am sky I am desire When I enter the batey with my ancestors

I am water I am drops I am rain I am ice When I enter the batey I flow with my ancestors

I am earth I am sand I am stoneI am soil When I enter the batey I connect with my ancestors

I am fireI am flameI am fire I am light When I enter the batey light of my ancestors

I am airI am wind I am breezeI am sky When I enter the batey I go with my ancestors

I am earthI am air I am water I am fire When I enter the batey I free myself

"Mom, I'm going to the bomba"
Chorus Mom, mom, I'm going to the bomba Give me your blessing Mom, mom, I'm going to the bomba  I am a cimarrón)
 when the drums sound

tags.tag: Mama me voy pa la bomba

children.text:
"Campo yo vivo triste" performed by Melody
Chorus
I live sad in the countryside
Every day suffering more
Oh God, what will become of me
If I don't dance this bomba
I am going to die
Verses
And it is the kindness that I consent, I want to sing to you the bomba of my Borinquen
That here I come to sing to you
I only ask that the drum beats and that the cua sounds too so that our ancestors up there in heaven can sing it.

"Familia de Alduén"  Maria Alduén
Chorus My family is present in the history of bomba. Everyone knows the Alduén family
Verse From Chicago to Mayagüez, my family is present in the history of bomba Everyone knows the Alduén family.
The drums sound, the elders also know, in history the family stands strong The west has the Alduén family. They dedicate themselves to the rhythm that will never be lost.
It was the voice of my great-uncle from heaven singing with my mother in flight. He from the clouds is there when I sing,  a rain of bomba that fills us with enchantment. 
 Today a voice tells me, little black girl, you will sing!

“Dominé,” Aliz María Castillo
Dominé, dominé
That Black woman, dominé, who works the snake
the way Rafael sings
Verses  
The way Rafael sings
This Black woman dances bomba
This Black woman, dominé, dominé
This Black woman works the snake like Rafael
She works the snake the way Rafael sings
This Black woman dances bomba
and stings the ground with her feet
Dominé, dominé, that Black woman, dominé
She works the snake the way Rafael sings
She works the snake the way Rafael sings
Quick mention of Neli (a bombera) who is recently diseased.

"Radiante Flor" Chorus  
My songs are the flowers
My memory is their fragrance
Verses  
Fragrant flower, life is fragile
But the spirit does not fade
It grows and rejoices in the legacy I see
Chorus  
My songs are the flowers
My memory is their fragrance
My songs are the flowers
My memory is their fragrance
Verse 
So that you remember me
I leave you melodies in abundance
With the color of my voice
The bougainvillea will dress itself
Chorus
My verses are the colors
They are of dawn, making this day better
Come with me, let me be with you, be with you
Look—petals of many colors
A bee is stinging me
And I’ve got the stinger
I took it out—let’s keep going here with the bomba
Your petals are of many colors
Chorus  
My voice extends
And it always accompanies you
My songs are the flowers
My memory in their fragrance

Remember me in your prayers
From the earth and the ancestors
May everyone dance
Come with me, let me be with you, be with you

"Yo soy del Oeste" Verse 
Over the Caribbean Sea, emblem of enchantment,
my heart beats like a drum at sunset.
With the setting sun from the cardinal points,
in the West I was born.

My ancestry comes to me, my lineage of warriors,
my essence and my honor.
From the cardinal points,
in the West I was born.
I am from the West like the strength of the sun.

Chorus  
Over the Caribbean Sea my heart beats.
I am from the West like the gate of the sun.
Over the Caribbean Sea my heart beats.

Verse  
I sing to my joyful plains,
my perfumed little cave, the home of my ancestress,
the one who gave me life.

I am from the West like the gate of the sun.
Over the Caribbean Sea my heart beats.

With the sway of the waves of its beach and its sea,
with its palms and mangroves, I will never stop loving.
I am from the West like the strength of the sun.
I am the Caribbean Sea, my heart in bloom.

Honoring the gift of that heritage—
of drumbeats and barrels, maracas and cua, hearing the rhythm.

In the West, like the setting sun,
over the Caribbean Sea my heart beats.

To the City of the Hills I dedicate a special verse,
founding city, that town my great brother.

I am from the West like the strength of the sun.
I conjure the murmur of the river, my native homeland,
its poppy petal and the fierce waves of the sea.

I am from the West like the gate of the sun.
Over the Caribbean Sea my heart beats.

The clearest and deepest blue I have ever seen
is found in the West of my ancestral Caribbean.

I am from the West like the strength of the sun.
Over the Caribbean Sea my heart beats.

Now in this other West, there in my spoken city,
my beloved San Francisco and the Mission, my community.

I am like the strength of the sun.
Over the Caribbean Sea my heart beats.

I am from your rugged coast, from your unmatched mountains,
from your celestial work of art.

I am from the West like the strength of the sun.
Over the Caribbean Sea my heart beats.

You have always given me the kisses of sun and sea,
the guava pulp and the flower of the camboy.

I am from the West like the setting sun.
Over the Caribbean Sea my heart beats.
I am from the West like the setting sun.
Over the Caribbean Sea my heart beats.

"Candela Colorao" A thunder is heard from far away
Like the lord of his own manifestation
And with quickened steps
I go on singing this song
And with quickened steps
In the rain I will sing
I came to the battle — red fire, red fire  
I did not come to the battle — red fire, red fire
If there is no guano in my house
I burn it

Chorus
Red fire, red fire
If in my land there is no guano
It is placed

"Goza de la bomba" Ausuba Verse  
Ah, enjoy it — enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
If you don’t know who’s singing to you, it’s Ausuba who’s playing for you.
Enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
Enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.

Chorus  
Go — enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
With Mariela, nothing less — that’s where you enjoy the bomba.
Enjoy, enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
Ay, enjoy it — enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
Enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
And with the rhythm from Joel, I’m off.
You enjoy the bomba of the sea.
With the bomba, you enjoy it.

Chorus  
Enjoy it — enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
Ay, enjoy, enjoy, enjoy, enjoy.
With Loíza and the singer,
Enjoy it — enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.

Verses  
Never prettier than the one who dances on the batey.
Now, now it’s time — enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
If you don’t know who’s singing to you,
Enjoy the bomba — if you don’t know who’s singing, it’s Toa.

Chorus  
Enjoy it — enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
Enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
Enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
Now’s the time — enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
Let them dance — enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
With Loíza and the singer,
Go — the bomba, enjoy it.
Ay, enjoy, enjoy, enjoy, enjoy.
Enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.
Enjoy the bomba, enjoy it.

"I was born a woman" Ausuba Chorus  
I was born a woman  
Strong and a warrior  
Free and beautiful  
And adventurous  
Verses
Always with my hands in this earth  
I am the molasses  
I am the mint  
I am a Taíno woman  
I am a Black woman  
I am a white woman  
That is my mixture  
Fruit of that union of beautiful races  
I am Antillean  
I am Caribbean  
And in pants I am still a maiden  
I was born a woman  
Strong and a warrior  
Fire and earth  
Wind and sea  
Steady and brave  
I am calm and peace  
Daughter of the great revolutionary  
Mother of the men who built a homeland

"Yo no estoy sola" Ausuba Chorus  
I am not alone I live my life in colors
Giving love to all the hearts
I am not alone I live my life in colors

Verses  
To the rhythm of the buleador in time the dancers hear it
Because they are about to dance with my life in colors
Giving love to all the hearts

To all the hearts giving love to all the hearts
I live my life in colors giving love to all the hearts

For everyone who loves me I dedicate this song
To everyone who loves me I offer it with love
Spreading love to all the hearts

And to all the hearts to all the hearts who dance
Dancers I gift you this song
Dance it with love to all the hearts

Hey dance dance now
To all the hearts this song goes
All the hearts dance it from the heart
To all the hearts from the heart to all the hearts

And dance it with love to all the hearts
So the dancers can dance it
To all the hearts who make the
Where is it to all the hearts

Dedication  
For everyone who loves me to all the hearts
I dedicate the song to all who love me
I give my heart to all the hearts

I am not alone I live my life in colors
Giving love to all the hearts

"Caminando" Ausuba
I keep on walking through life
Walking, marking a steady step
And I keep on walking and I keep on walking
Walking through life, walking
Marking a steady step
And I keep on walking and I keep on walking
Through life, walking, marking a steady step
And I keep on walking and I keep on walking
Through life, walking, marching with a steady step

Walking day by day, many times I found myself
And along that path I faced a thousand things
And I keep on walking with life
Walking, marking a steady step
And I keep on walking with a steady step

I keep on walking, brushing off
Anything that tries to take my bread away
And I keep on drumming and I keep on walking
With life, walking, marking a steady step

Some people ask me
How I keep moving
I just follow the heart
So the wind won’t carry me away

And I keep on walking with life
Walking, marking a steady step
And I keep on walking little by little through life
I will keep singing the cheles while walking
Walking to the top — I will get there

And I keep on walking through life
Walking, marking a steady step

Some people ask me why I keep moving
I just follow the heart so the wind won’t carry me away

And I keep on walking through life
Walking, marking a steady step
And I keep on walking with life
Walking, marking a steady step

And I keep on walking, sweating the big drop
What a sweet warmth from Loíza, from the love

Dedication
So we’re good — this tasty song
From Chicago to Puerto Rico I dedicate it
To all the beautiful people here today
And congratulations to all the cantadoras
From our elders to those who started as babies

This role of the singer is still important
Because that’s where the history is — that’s where we learn so much

Thank you, Usua, for the invitation to share with you

I have arrived, I have arrived, I have arrived walking
I have arrived, I have arrived, I have arrived walking

Step by step I will get there
Let the seed I planted guide me

I have arrived, I have arrived walking
From Chicago I come singing
Oh I arrived, oh yes I arrived
I have arrived, I have arrived, I have arrived walking
I have arrived, I have arrived, I have arrived walking

"Por que me dicen morena?" Ausuba Someone shouted from far away, “Morena, come over here.”
How beautiful that hair is, it looks spectacular on you.
Then I said, “Morena? Thank you, you’re very kind.
But I want to correct something, and I hope you take it well.
It’s just that I am not morena, nor am I the color of coffee.
I am proudly Black, and that is what I will always be.
Why do you call me morena if that is not my color?
I have a Black heritage, and I am proud of it.
Verses  
From my ancestors and from the sound of my drum,
I am Black by birth, a singer at heart.
Because the race lives in me — that’s why Black, Black is what I am.
Why do you call me morena if that is not my color?
I have a Black heritage, and I am proud of it.
Oh, I have a pure lineage — warriors and compassion,
Who rose up in struggle and defended our color.
I have a Black heritage, and I am proud of it.
My race is my color, and I am proud of it.

"Igualida, Libertad, y  Respecto" Ausuba
We always remember that the importance of love is mutual  
That freedom is also mutual  
And that there is love for all women, for all men, and for everyone  
As long as it is so  
I didn’t come to be fooled, I have a voice, I have a right  
I come to fight for what I want  
Equality, freedom, and respect  
I didn’t come to be fooled, I have a voice, I have a right  
I come to fight for what I want  
Equality, freedom, and respect  
We are no longer the submissive ones  
Today I shout it to the four winds  
I have a voice, I have a right  
Equality, freedom, and respect  
I didn’t come to be fooled, I have a voice, I have a right  
I come to fight for what I want  
Equality, freedom, and respect  
And there is no more time for parliament  
Nor for thinking about remorse  
A movement rises  
I do not submit to the government  
I didn’t come to be fooled, I have a voice, I have a right  
I come to fight for what I want  
Equality, freedom, and respect.

"No me vas a pegar" Ausuba
So what happens if you catch me with someone else
If I catch you flirting with another
All I demand is respectful treatment
And that your love be steady and respectful
And what happens if you catch me with someone else
If I see you flirting with another
You are not going to hit me
Nor manipulate me nor mistreat me
Nothing happens if you see me flirting with someone else
You are not going to do anything to me
If I see you flirting with another
What you are is a macho bully
And what happens if you catch me with someone else
If I see you flirting with another
And what happens if you catch me with someone else
If I see you flirting with another
Don’t you come to hit me
Don’t you come to hit me
Don’t come to mistreat me
Don’t you come to hit me
Brother you are not going to manipulate me
Don’t you come to hit me
Much less mistreat me
Don’t you come to hit me
